title: Fallout 3 - Game of the Year Edition
notes: You have to disable Multimonitor-Setups. If the Game starts with only one Monitor it works :] Also needed to manually add the xlive.dll in the installation Folder.

title: Fallout 3 - Game of the Year Edition
notes:
Runs if xlive.dll is installed, I installed it by downloading http://download.gfwl.xboxlive.com/content/gfwl-public/redists/production/xliveredist.msi
then running `/tmp/proton_run msiexec /i /path/to/xliveredist.msi`

title: Fallout 3 - Game of the Year Edition
notes: xlive.dll was necessary, taken from C:\Windows\SysWOW64\xlive.dll from a native win7 system.  Game crashed after exit from vault 101, right as first level up window popped.  Reloading from save game resulted in black textures but level up succeeded. Successive reloads brought textures back.  Upon exiting into the wastes, any time you point your character in the direction of Vault 101, the entire texturing changes, your hands/weapon disappear, as does the green tint.  First time I attempt to buy from Crow, game crashes.

title: Fallout 3 Game of the Year Edition
notes: Copied Xlive.dll to the game folder before ever starting. The game runs fine, but sometimes it will randomly freeze or the window will disappear when tabbing out.

title: Fallout 3 GOTY
notes:
- Requires quartz and lcodecx to be installed with protontricks `protontricks 22370 l3codecx quartz`
- Requires xliveredist.msi to be installed with `/tmp/proton_run msiexec /i ${ABSOLUTE_PATH}/xliveredist.msi`
- Requires that all water effects are disabled to avoid serious rendering glitches

title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Sometimes won't start, radio station won't play on pip-boy, but will play on external radio.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Had to obtain a copy of xlive.dll, found some random copy from one of those random dll download sites as I have no Windows machines available. Just place the xlive.dll file into ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Fallout 3 goty/ and you are good to go.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: First I did install xact via winetricks, but the black screen still appears. Not sure if xact is really necessary. So, I tried to install the xliveredist.msi, and copy paste the xlive.dll to the game install root. Then the game looks to run fine in windowed mode.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Copied xlive.dll to solve the black screen. Edited ...\Documents\My Games\Fallout3\FALLOUT.ini and changed bUseThreadedAI=0 to 1 and added iNumHWThreads=2 line to solve random crashes. Alt-tabbing to another window makes the game crash.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs fine after adding xlive.dll and doing the aforementioned changes in FALLOUT.ini. Make sure you edit the correct ini-file, search for it in %steam%/steamapps/compatdata/22370/pfx/.../My Games/Fallout3
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:

Adding this did not work either, the screen was first black and then it did not appear again. 'bUseThreadedAI=1'
'iNumHWThreads=2'
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: added xlive.dll to game folder and disabled second screen
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs fine at first, then it drags down to 9fps as soon as I enter the Atrium. Exiting the vault does not fix this, or restarting the game.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Steps: Install from Steam. Do not open the game. Paste xlive.dll into /home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Fallout 3 goty. Leave the predefined options of the game.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Opens a window that stops responding
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Game runs pretty smoothly and all features seem to work after some tweaking. I get the occasional frame drop, but seems normal from what I remember playing on Windows 7. I haven't tested with many mods since I can't seem to get a mod manager working, but mods and FOSE work fine. Just rename fose_loader.exe to FalloutLauncher.exe.

Rather than downloading GFWL, I instead downloaded the Games for Windows LIVE Disabler from NexusMods. You'll have to run the game's launcher and set your settings to generate an ini before the Disabler will let you run it.
To get steam to install/run the GFWL Disabler, I copied it to the game directory, renamed FalloutLauncher.exe to something else, then renamed the Disabler to FalloutLauncher.exe to trick steam into running it (I'm sure there's a proper way to do this, but I'm not sure what that is). You shouldn't have to Move DLCs, but you may need to enable them in the launcher.

As others have mentioned, these lines need to be edited/added in the [GENERAL] section to fix crashing:
bThreadedAI=1
iNumHWThreads=2

The mouse acceleration is also terrible, so if you don't use the unofficial patch I recommend turning it off by adding these lines in the [CONTROLS] section:
fForegroundMouseAccelBase=0
fForegroundMouseAccelTop=0
fForegroundMouseBase=0
fForegroundMouseMult=0

As mentioned below me, to fix the radio, you can use `protontricks 22370 l3codecx quartz`

Lastly, I had some texture flickering (white spots) and artifacts from the advanced water effects. Turning off AA fixed these.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs well out of the box, although it's worth disabling mouse acceleration in the ini files.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Added xlive.dll to bypass the black screen bug, once in game managed to escape the vault with minor graphical glitches. Framerate overall is playable on my system. Once escaping the vault, a lot of particle flickers and a lot of texture flickering at further distance. Haven't applied the iNumHWThreads and bUseTHreadedAi to the ini file as of yet but my game hasn't crashed thus far. Will play around further with the files.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

cpu: AMD A8-6500 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Copied xlive.dll from the wineroot which was created by running the game once, in $STEAM_DIR/SteamApps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/windows/syswow64/, into the game's root dir, to solve the black-screen issue.  Did the suggested INI fixes in $STEAM_DIR/SteamApps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout3/FALLOUT.INI, namely setting "bUseThreadedAI=1" and adding "iNumHWThreads=2" in the [General] section, and adding these to the [Controls] section: fForegroundMouseAccelBase=0, fForegroundMouseAccelTop=0, fForegroundMouseBase=0, and fForegroundMouseMult=0.  Also ran "protontricks 22370 l3codecx quartz", which apparently allows the pip boy radio to work.  After all that, I've had no problems.  Only played up through getting out of Bunker 101 so far, though.  The game definitely resists being alt-tabbed out of, but I can generally do some virtual-desktop switching to get it to properly background, and then go back to it later.  Have done that a number of times so far without crashes, though sometimes you'll have to try a few times 'cause it might continue to lock the mouse after being backgrounded.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

cpu: AMD A10-7700K APU with Radeon R7 Graphics
notes: I installed all tricks and configs listed on this and other sites.  Game loads and runs, I can play for random time.  But at first combat or second combat it freezes and locks. Everything else works.  I do not know why it locks up.  I edited ini files, add xlive.dll, and I even tried to run only 1 core but it had no impact on game seizing.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Works perfectly after copying xlive.dll (got from(http://download.gfwl.xboxlive.com/content/gfwl-public/redists/production/xliveredist.msi, installing on Windows, and copying the DLL from C:\Windows\SysWOW64\xlive.dll) then editing the Fallout.ini (located at /home/USERNAME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout3/FALLOUT.INI) you need to add these lines under [General] bUseThreadedAI=1
iNumHWThreads=2 otherwise it will crash randomly. I have set my GFX to the highest it will go to, and played a good hour inside the vault, and just gotten out the vault and it works flawlessly so far after said tweaks.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Besides applying necessary tweaks, such as coping xlive.dll and editing FALLOUT.INI, I also applied reg-file published in bugreport about Fallout 3: GOTY in Proton GitHub account. Applying this reg-file was necessary in my case to get game working on Intel GPU.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs without Problems WHEN (!!!) you disable Antialising and Antisotrophic Filtering. It caused Glitches and Problems in V.A.T.S on my System (i always got 0% in V.A.T.S with active AA and Antisotrophic Filtering).
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition
